RequestErrorCode.title: RequestErrorCode
RequestErrorCode.description: Standardized error codes for API responses. The `*_OTHER_ERROR` family (`CREATE_ORDER_OTHER_ERROR`, `CANCEL_ORDER_OTHER_ERROR`, `CANCEL_ALL_AFTER_OTHER_ERROR`) is the per-operation catch-all for a matching-engine-side failure that has no more specific code — the human-readable `message` carries the detail. Modify-specific failures surface as `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR` (bad/immutable restate), `ORDER_NOT_FOUND_ERROR`, `EMPTY_MODIFY_ERROR`, `MODIFY_QTY_BELOW_FILLED_ERROR`, `POST_ONLY_WOULD_CROSS_ERROR`, or `MODIFY_ORDER_OTHER_ERROR`. Branch-able failure reasons lifted out of the `*_OTHER_ERROR` catch-alls so clients can pick a retry/UX strategy without parsing the free-text `message`: `RATE_LIMITED_ERROR` (request throttled — back off and retry; create/modify/cancel), `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE_ERROR` (collateral too low for the order; create/modify), `OPEN_ORDER_CAP_ERROR` (the account's resting GTC open-order limit is reached; create), `PRICE_QTY_BOUNDS_ERROR` (price or quantity outside the market's accepted bounds; create/modify), `SERVICE_DISABLED_ERROR` (order entry is disabled for this market/instrument — operational kill-switch or market not enabled; create/modify), `UNAUTHORIZED_ACCOUNT_ERROR` (a valid signer that does not own the target order — distinct from `UNAUTHORIZED_SIGNATURE_ERROR`, which is a bad/unauthorized signature; cancel/modify), `TRADING_HALTED_ERROR` (the market is halted; create), and `DUPLICATE_CLIENT_ORDER_ID_ERROR` (the `clientOrderId` is already in use by a live order; create).

ServerErrorCode.title: ServerErrorCode
ServerErrorCode.description: Standardized error codes for API responses

TimeInForce.title: TimeInForce
TimeInForce.description: Order time in force (IOC = Immediate or Cancel, GTC = Good Till Cancel, GTT = Good Till Time)

OrderType.title: OrderType
OrderType.description: Order type aligned with the on-chain `OrderDetails.orderType` enum: LIMIT = limit order, STOP_LOSS = stop-loss trigger order, TAKE_PROFIT = take-profit trigger order.

AccountType.title: AccountType
AccountType.description: SPOT = account that can only trade spot, MAINPERP = main perp account, SUBPERP = sub perp account

CancelReason.title: CancelReason
CancelReason.description: Why an order reached a terminal `CANCELLED` status. Present only when `status` is `CANCELLED`. `NO_LIQUIDITY` (IOC found no resting liquidity) and `IOC_REMAINDER` (IOC partially filled and the remaining quantity was cancelled because no more executable opposite liquidity was available) are returned on `createOrder` / `modifyOrder` responses; `GTT_EXPIRED`, `USER_CANCEL`, `MASS_CANCEL` and `CANCEL_ALL_AFTER` are delivered on the `walletOrderChanges` stream. `SELF_TRADE_PREVENTION` (the order would have matched your own resting order) cancels the **aggressor (taker) only** - your resting order is left untouched, so there is no resting-side STP event. It appears on the response when a self-crossing IOC or modify cancels the taker, and on the `walletOrderChanges` stream when a self-crossing GTC aggressor is cancelled. If an IOC partially fills and its remaining quantity is then cancelled by self-trade prevention, the response keeps the fill quantities and reports `cancelReason: SELF_TRADE_PREVENTION`; `IOC_REMAINDER` is used only when the remaining quantity is cancelled because matching stops without a self-cross.

TierType.title: TierType
TierType.description: Fee tier type (REGULAR = Standard tier, VIP = VIP tier)

DepthType.title: DepthType
DepthType.description: Depth message type (SNAPSHOT = full book, UPDATE = single level change)

updatedAt.description: Time when the market summary was last calculated (milliseconds)

longFundingValue.description: Current reference value of funding accrued by one unit of exposure; there is one funding value per market and per direction, with short v long funding values differing possibly due to Auto-Deleveraging (ADL)

shortFundingValue.description: Current reference value of funding accrued by one unit of exposure; there is one funding value per market and per direction, with short v long funding values differing possibly due to Auto-Deleveraging (ADL)

markPrice.description: Last mark price, at the moment of the last market summary update

throttledMidPrice.description: Last mid price, at the moment of the last market summary update

pricesUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last price update (milliseconds)

updatedAt.description: Time when the spot market summary was last calculated (milliseconds)

avgEntryFundingValue.description: Average of funding values at the entry times of currently open exposure, which serves as a baseline from which to compute the accrued funding in the position: units x (fundingValue - avgEntryFundingValue)

lastTradeSequenceNumber.description: Sequence number of last execution taken into account for the position.

execQty.description: Executed quantity in the current order update.

cumQty.description: Total Executed quantity accross all fills where the order is involved.

firstFillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce of the first fill this update represents. Together with fillCount it identifies the fills as a contiguous nonce range [firstFillId, firstFillId + fillCount - 1]. For a taker update, the first fill of its matching round; for a maker update, its single fill. Present only on fill updates; absent for non-fill updates and resting-order snapshots.

fillCount.description: Number of fills this update represents — the length of the contiguous nonce range starting at firstFillId. For a taker update, the fills produced in its matching round; for a maker update, 1. Present only on fill updates; absent otherwise (so it is >= 1 whenever present).

triggerPx.description: Price at which TP/SL orders will be triggered, should not be set for other order types.

timeInForce.description: Order time in force, exclusively used for LIMIT orders

expiresAfter.description: Unix seconds. On-chain order expiry (`OrderDetails.expiresAfter`); `0` or absent means good-until-cancelled. Non-zero only for GTT orders. Updated by `modifyOrder`.

reduceOnly.description: Whether this is a reduce-only order, exclusively used for LIMIT IOC orders.

postOnly.description: Whether this is a post-only (maker-only) order. Mirrors `CreateOrderRequest.postOnly`; updated by `modifyOrder`.

cancelReason.description: Machine-readable reason the order was cancelled. Present only when `status` is `CANCELLED`; absent otherwise.

cancelReasonMessage.description: Human-readable explanation of `cancelReason`. Present only when `cancelReason` is present.
cancelReasonMessage.example: Order cancelled because it would have matched your own resting order

realBalance.description: Sum of account net deposits (transfers, deposits and withdrawals) and realized pnl from closed positions. Realized pnl only applies to RUSD given it is the only settlement asset

balanceDEPRECATED.description: Sum of account net deposits (transfers, deposits and withdrawals). This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release

takerOrderId.description: Order ID for the taker. Absent for legacy V2 executions and omitted when not meaningful.

makerOrderId.description: Order ID for the maker. Absent for legacy V2 executions and omitted when not meaningful.

side.description: Execution side from the taker perspective for ORDER_MATCH executions.

takerFee.description: Signed taker fee impact in rUSD. Positive means the taker paid a fee.

makerFee.description: Signed maker fee impact in rUSD. Negative means rebate. Absent for legacy V2 executions.

takerOpeningFee.description: Opening fee portion of the taker fee in rUSD. Absent for position-extending executions.

makerOpeningFee.description: Opening fee portion of the maker fee in rUSD. Absent for position-extending executions and legacy V2 executions.

sequenceNumber.description: Execution sequence number, increases by 1 for every perp execution in reya chain

fillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce — a stable identifier to join this execution to its ME fill (PRO-182).

takerRealizedPnl.description: Realized taker PnL from this execution in rUSD (takerPriceVariationPnl + takerFundingPnl). Absent for position-extending executions.

makerRealizedPnl.description: Realized maker PnL from this execution in rUSD (makerPriceVariationPnl + makerFundingPnl). Absent when counterparty state is unavailable or the execution is position-extending.

takerPriceVariationPnl.description: Taker PnL component from price movement in rUSD. Absent for position-extending executions.

makerPriceVariationPnl.description: Maker PnL component from price movement in rUSD. Absent when counterparty state is unavailable or the execution is position-extending.

takerFundingPnl.description: Taker PnL component from funding payments in rUSD. Absent for position-extending executions.

makerFundingPnl.description: Maker PnL component from funding payments in rUSD. Absent when counterparty state is unavailable or the execution is position-extending.

sequenceNumber.description: Execution sequence number, increases by 1 for every spot execution in reya chain

fillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce — a stable identifier to join this execution to its ME fill (PRO-182).

symbol.description: Market symbol. Spot symbols end in RUSD (e.g. ETHRUSD); perp symbols end in RUSDPERP (e.g. BTCRUSDPERP). Clients can use the suffix to distinguish spot vs perp.

reason.description: Human Readable Reason String (decoded revert reason bytes)

sequenceNumber.description: Execution sequence number, increases by 1 for every spot execution bust in reya chain

fillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce — a stable identifier to join this bust to its ME fill (PRO-182).

Price.title: Price
Price.description: Deprecated AMM-era mixed market/collateral price shape. Use AssetOraclePrice for asset Stork oracle prices; use market summary/depth endpoints for market prices.

oraclePrice.description: Price given by the Stork feeds, used both as the peg price for prices on Reya, as well as Mark Prices. The Stork price feed is usually the perp prices across three major CEXs

poolPrice.description: The price currently quoted by the AMM for zero volume, from which trades are priced (equivalent to mid price in an order book); a trade of any size will be move this price up or down depending on the direction.

oraclePrice.description: Asset Stork oracle price. No AMM/order-book `poolPrice` is included on this replacement feed.

marketId.description: Numerical identifier for each market, only needed to generate signatures

liquidationMarginParameter.description: Minimum percentage of notional that needs to be covered to avoid liquidation procedures for a given market; below this value, your account is subject to liquidation procedures. When cross margining, all requirements across markets are covered by the same balance, and all positions are subject to liquidations.

initialMarginParameter.description: Minimum percentage of notional that needs to be covered post trade; if the account does not satisfy this requirement, trades will not get executed.

oiCap.description: Maximum one-sided open interest in units for a given market.

marketId.description: Numerical identifier for each spot market

priceHaircut.description: Notional discount to the value of a collateral when used to satisfy the margin requirements; it does not imply any token conversion, but is rather an accounting adjustment.

liquidationDiscount.description: Discount in the token price when liquidating collateral.

status.description: Status of asset (ENABLED = deposits and withdrawals allowed, WITHDRAWAL_ONLY = only withdrawals allowed)

decimals.description: Number of decimal places for record keeping amounts of this asset (e.g. 18 for ETH, 6 for RUSD)

displayDecimals.description: Number of decimal places shown for display purposes in frontends

takerFee.description: Taker fee rate (fee will be qty * takerFee)

makerFee.description: Maker fee rate (fee will be qty * makerFee)

volume14d.description: 14-day volume level required this fee tier to be applied to a wallet

endTime.description: Timestamp of last result, in milliseconds

startTime.description: Timestamp of first result, in milliseconds

CreateOrderRequest.title: CreateOrderRequest
CreateOrderRequest.description: Order creation request. The fields carried here mirror the on-chain `OrderDetails` struct that the client signs via EIP-712. The REST surface keeps `isBuy` + `qty` as separate fields for symmetry with the rest of the API (Order, Execution, Trade schemas); on the signing side the signed `OrderDetails.quantity` is reconstructed as the signed int256 `isBuy ? +qty : -qty`. Two distinct time-related fields are carried and signed: `deadline` (signature validity — enforced by the API at entry) and `expiresAfter` (order lifetime — enforced on-chain at execution). Convention: if `expiresAfter > 0`, clients should ensure `deadline <= expiresAfter` to avoid signing a dead-on-arrival order. See `docs/eip712.md` for the signing algorithm and exact typehash strings.

symbol.description: Market symbol. The server resolves this to the on-chain `OrderDetails.marketId`, which uses a unified namespace: perp market ids are raw core ids, spot market ids are `coreMarketId + 1e10`.

isBuy.description: Whether this is a buy order. Combined with `qty`, determines the signed `OrderDetails.quantity` (int256): positive for buy/long, negative for sell/short.

limitPx.description: Worst acceptable execution price. Must be a positive number (> 0). Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.limitPrice` (uint256).

qty.description: Unsigned order quantity. Combined with `isBuy`, forms the signed `OrderDetails.quantity`.

timeInForce.description: Order time in force (exclusively used for LIMIT orders). Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.timeInForce` (0 = GTC, 1 = IOC, 2 = GTT). This field is signed but not enforced on-chain.

triggerPx.description: Trigger price, required and non-zero for STOP_LOSS/TAKE_PROFIT, must be zero/absent for LIMIT. Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.triggerPrice`.

reduceOnly.description: Reduce-only intent. Perp only; spot markets must set this to false. Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.reduceOnly`.

postOnly.description: Post-only (maker-only) intent: the order must rest and never cross as a taker. Valid on GTC/GTT; rejected on IOC. An order that would cross at insertion is rejected with `POST_ONLY_WOULD_CROSS_ERROR`. Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.postOnly`.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature over the `Order(uint256 verifyingChainId, uint256 deadline, OrderDetails order)` envelope. See `docs/eip712.md` for the exact typehash string and signing algorithm.

nonce.description: Monotonically increasing per-signer nonce. Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.nonce`.

signerWallet.description: Address of the signer that produced the EIP-712 signature. Maps to on-chain `OrderDetails.signer`.

deadline.description: Unix seconds. EIP-712 signature validity deadline — signed into the `Order` envelope and enforced by the API at entry. Requests received after this timestamp are rejected before the order reaches the matching engine.

expiresAfter.description: Unix seconds. Order lifetime — signed into `OrderDetails.expiresAfter` and enforced on-chain at execution. `0` (the default if omitted) means good-until-cancelled. Coupled to `timeInForce`; mismatched combinations are rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`: GTC and IOC MUST use `0` (a non-zero value is rejected — GTC never expires), and GTT MUST set a non-zero `expiresAfter` greater than `deadline` (`deadline <= expiresAfter`). Note: a GTT fill matched within a few seconds of `expiresAfter` can revert on-chain with `OrderExpired` if it mines after expiry, so choose a lifetime with adequate margin.

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID for tracking and correlation, as a decimal string. Maps to the on-chain `OrderDetails.clientOrderId` (`uint64`) — signed as part of the EIP-712 payload but not enforced on-chain. Carried as a string (full `uint64` range [0, 2^64-1]) to preserve precision, like `orderId` and `nonce`.

execQty.description: Executed quantity in the current order update.

cumQty.description: Total Executed quantity accross all fills where the order is involved.

orderId.description: Engine-assigned order ID, generated for all order types including IOC. A no-cross IOC still receives an ID and is returned with status CANCELLED (it never rests).

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID echoed back from the request, as a decimal string (`uint64`).

cancelReason.description: Machine-readable reason the order was cancelled. Present only when `status` is `CANCELLED` — e.g. a no-cross IOC returns `status: CANCELLED` with `cancelReason: NO_LIQUIDITY`, and a self-crossing IOC with `SELF_TRADE_PREVENTION`. Absent otherwise.

cancelReasonMessage.description: Human-readable explanation of `cancelReason`. Present only when `cancelReason` is present.
cancelReasonMessage.example: Order cancelled: no resting liquidity to fill against

firstFillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce of the first fill this order produced on entry. Together with fillCount it identifies the fills as a contiguous nonce range [firstFillId, firstFillId + fillCount - 1]. Absent if the order did not fill on entry. For a non-IOC (resting) taker, the same first nonce also appears on the order's taker update in the orderChanges channel; an IOC taker is not published to orderChanges, so this response is the only place its fill range is delivered.

fillCount.description: Number of fills this order produced on entry — the length of the contiguous nonce range starting at firstFillId. Absent if the order did not fill on entry (so it is >= 1 whenever present).

ModifyOrderRequest.title: ModifyOrderRequest
ModifyOrderRequest.description: Modifies a resting order in place. The order keeps its `orderId` and `clientOrderId`. Target exactly one of `orderId` / `clientOrderId`; supplying both or neither is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`, and a target that does not resolve to a live resting order is rejected with `ORDER_NOT_FOUND_ERROR`. **Full restate:** the request carries the SAME fields as `createOrder` (the complete post-modify `OrderDetails`) plus the target id — restate every value at its post-modify state, even unchanged ones; there is no omitted-means-inherited shorthand. The five modifiable fields are `limitPx`, `qty`, `postOnly`, `expiresAfter`, and `triggerPx` — for a `STOP_LOSS` / `TAKE_PROFIT` order the trigger price can be re-priced in place (no cancel+recreate). The remaining signed `OrderDetails` fields (`exchangeId`, `isBuy`/side, `orderType`, `timeInForce`, `reduceOnly`, `clientOrderId`, `accountId`, `signerWallet`) are IMMUTABLE: restate each at the resting order's value — the matching engine verifies each equals the resting order and rejects a mismatch with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR` (the matching engine's internal `MODIFY_IMMUTABLE_MISMATCH` is surfaced to clients as `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`; the human-readable `message` names the offending field). `orderType` is immutable — a modify re-prices a trigger but never converts an order between `LIMIT` and `STOP_LOSS` / `TAKE_PROFIT`. **Availability:** editing a `STOP_LOSS` / `TAKE_PROFIT` order (including its `triggerPx`) depends on native SL/TP trigger orders landing in the matching engine; until then, modify accepts only `LIMIT` orders and a `STOP_LOSS` / `TAKE_PROFIT` modify is rejected with `MODIFY_ORDER_OTHER_ERROR`. A `LIMIT` order's `triggerPx` is `0`. The EIP-712 signature is verified over exactly the restated fields (the same `Order` / `OrderDetails` envelope as `createOrder`), so it needs no resting-order lookup; a fresh `nonce` is required. A request whose post-modify state is identical to the order's current state is rejected with `EMPTY_MODIFY_ERROR`. Queue priority: a `qty` decrease at an unchanged `limitPx` preserves the order's place in the book; any `limitPx` change or `qty` increase loses it (re-queued at the new level). `qty` is the TOTAL order quantity, not remaining, and must be strictly greater than the order's `cumQty` (else `MODIFY_QTY_BELOW_FILLED_ERROR`). `timeInForce` is immutable — a modify cannot flip GTC↔GTT; the restated `timeInForce` must match the resting order and `expiresAfter` must stay consistent with it (`0` for GTC, a non-zero future timestamp for GTT). If the post-modify order is post-only and would cross, the modification is rejected with `POST_ONLY_WOULD_CROSS_ERROR` and the resting order is left untouched, priority intact. If it is not post-only and the new `limitPx` crosses, the modification executes immediately — the response reports the executed quantity (`execQty`) and resulting `status`, with per-fill detail delivered on the wallet executions and `walletOrderChanges` streams, exactly as for `createOrder`. See `docs/eip712.md` for the signing algorithm and exact typehash strings.

orderId.description: Internal matching engine order ID of the order to modify. Exactly one of `orderId` or `clientOrderId` must be provided.

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID of the order to modify (the same `clientOrderId` supplied in CreateOrderRequest), as a decimal string (`uint64`). Exactly one of `orderId` or `clientOrderId` must be provided. The value `"0"` is the "no tag" sentinel and is NOT a valid sole target — an order created without a client order id can only be modified by `orderId`. Supplying both `orderId` and `clientOrderId`, or neither, is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`. The modification cannot assign a new `clientOrderId`; the resting order's value is preserved.

accountId.description: Account ID that owns the order. Immutable; must match the resting order.

exchangeId.description: On-chain `OrderDetails.exchangeId`. Immutable — restate the resting order's value (a mismatch is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`).

isBuy.description: Order side. Immutable — restate the resting order's value. Combined with `qty`, sets the signed `OrderDetails.quantity` (int256). A mismatch is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`.

orderType.description: On-chain `OrderDetails.orderType`. Immutable — restate the resting order's value; a modify never converts between `LIMIT` and `STOP_LOSS`/`TAKE_PROFIT`. A mismatch is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`. Availability: modifying a `STOP_LOSS`/`TAKE_PROFIT` order depends on native SL/TP trigger orders landing in the matching engine; until then only `LIMIT` orders are modifiable and a `STOP_LOSS`/`TAKE_PROFIT` modify is rejected with `MODIFY_ORDER_OTHER_ERROR`.

timeInForce.description: On-chain `OrderDetails.timeInForce` (0 = GTC, 2 = GTT for resting orders). Immutable and REQUIRED — restate the resting order's value; a modify cannot flip GTC↔GTT. A mismatch is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`.

triggerPx.description: On-chain `OrderDetails.triggerPrice`. Modifiable — for a `STOP_LOSS`/`TAKE_PROFIT` order the trigger can be re-priced in place (no cancel+recreate); restate the post-modify value. `0` for a `LIMIT` order. The matching engine applies the restated value (it is not immutable-matched). Availability: depends on native SL/TP trigger orders landing in the matching engine (see `orderType`); until then a `LIMIT` order's `triggerPx` stays `0`. Optional on the wire — an absent `triggerPx` is treated as `0`.

reduceOnly.description: On-chain `OrderDetails.reduceOnly`. Immutable — restate the resting order's value. A mismatch is rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR`.

limitPx.description: The post-modify worst acceptable execution price. Must be a positive number (> 0); the off-chain validator and the matching engine both reject `<= 0`. Always required — send the complete intended value even when it is unchanged from the resting order. Changing it loses queue priority; a non-post-only modification whose new price crosses executes immediately.

qty.description: The post-modify TOTAL order quantity (not remaining). Always required — send the complete intended value even when it is unchanged from the resting order. Must be strictly greater than the order's `cumQty` (else `MODIFY_QTY_BELOW_FILLED_ERROR`). A decrease at an unchanged `limitPx` preserves queue priority; an increase loses it.

postOnly.description: The post-modify post-only (maker-only) flag. Always required — send the complete intended value even when it is unchanged from the resting order. If true and the post-modify order would cross, the modification is rejected with `POST_ONLY_WOULD_CROSS_ERROR` and the resting order is unchanged.

expiresAfter.description: Unix seconds. The post-modify order expiry (`OrderDetails.expiresAfter`). Always required — send the complete intended value even when it is unchanged from the resting order; `0` means no expiry and is a valid post-modify state. Must stay consistent with the order's `timeInForce` (`0` for GTC, non-zero for GTT): modifications that would flip an order between GTC and GTT are rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR` (`timeInForce` is not modifiable). For non-zero values, clients should ensure `deadline <= expiresAfter` to avoid signing a dead-on-arrival order (same convention as `createOrder`).

signature.description: Fresh EIP-712 signature over the full post-modify order state — the same `Order` envelope as `createOrder`, with the modified values substituted into `OrderDetails`. See `docs/eip712.md` for the exact typehash string and signing algorithm.

nonce.description: Monotonically increasing per-signer nonce. A fresh nonce is required for every modification; replayed nonces are rejected with `INVALID_NONCE_ERROR`.

signerWallet.description: Address of the signer that produced the EIP-712 signature. Not modifiable; must match the resting order's signer.

deadline.description: Unix seconds. EIP-712 signature validity deadline — signed into the `Order` envelope and enforced by the API at entry. Distinct from `expiresAfter` (order lifetime).

ModifyOrderResponse.title: ModifyOrderResponse
ModifyOrderResponse.description: Result of a modification, same shape as `CreateOrderResponse` plus `execQty` / `cumQty`. `orderId` is always the same ID the order had before the modification. If the modification crossed the book it executed immediately: `execQty` carries the quantity it filled and `status` reflects the outcome (`OPEN` for a partial fill leaving a remainder resting, `FILLED` for a complete fill, or `CANCELLED` when a non-post-only crossing modify self-matches the account's own resting liquidity — self-match prevention cancels the taker, so the order is removed and rests nowhere). Per-fill detail (prices, fees) is delivered on the wallet executions and `walletOrderChanges` streams, exactly as for `createOrder`.

execQty.description: Quantity executed by this modification (taker fills triggered when it crossed). `0` or absent when the modified order rested.

cumQty.description: Total executed quantity across the order's lifetime, including fills from before the modification.

orderId.description: Order ID — unchanged by the modification (the same ID the order had before).

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID preserved from order creation, as a decimal string (`uint64`).

cancelReason.description: Machine-readable reason the modified order was cancelled. Present only when `status` is `CANCELLED` — for a self-crossing non-post-only modify this is `SELF_TRADE_PREVENTION`. Absent otherwise.

cancelReasonMessage.description: Human-readable explanation of `cancelReason`. Present only when `cancelReason` is present.
cancelReasonMessage.example: Order cancelled because it would have matched your own resting order

firstFillId.description: Matching-engine fill nonce of the first fill this order produced on entry. Together with fillCount it identifies the fills as a contiguous nonce range [firstFillId, firstFillId + fillCount - 1]. Absent if the order did not fill on entry. For a non-IOC (resting) taker, the same first nonce also appears on the order's taker update in the orderChanges channel; an IOC taker is not published to orderChanges, so this response is the only place its fill range is delivered.

fillCount.description: Number of fills this order produced on entry — the length of the contiguous nonce range starting at firstFillId. Absent if the order did not fill on entry (so it is >= 1 whenever present).

orderId.description: Internal matching engine order ID to cancel. At least one of `orderId` or `clientOrderId` must be provided; if both are supplied the server treats `orderId` as the canonical identifier and `clientOrderId` is ignored. For spot markets, this is the order ID returned in the CreateOrderResponse.

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID for tracking and correlation, as a decimal string (`uint64`). At least one of `orderId` or `clientOrderId` must be provided; `clientOrderId` is consulted only when `orderId` is absent. This is the same clientOrderId provided in CreateOrderRequest.

accountId.description: Account ID that owns the order.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature over the `OrderCancel(uint64 verifyingChainId, uint64 deadline, OrderCancelDetails cancel)` envelope. See `docs/eip712.md` for the exact typehash string and signing algorithm.

nonce.description: Monotonically increasing per-signer nonce. A fresh nonce is required per request; replayed nonces are rejected with `INVALID_NONCE_ERROR`. See `docs/eip712.md`.

deadline.description: Unix seconds. EIP-712 signature validity deadline — signed into the `OrderCancel` envelope and enforced by the API at entry.

clientOrderId.description: Client-provided order ID echoed back from the request, as a decimal string (`uint64`).

MassCancelRequest.title: MassCancelRequest

accountId.description: Account ID to cancel orders for.

symbol.description: Symbol to cancel orders for. If not specified, cancels orders for all symbols.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature over the `MassCancel(uint64 verifyingChainId, uint64 deadline, MassCancelDetails massCancel)` envelope. See `docs/eip712.md` for the exact typehash string and signing algorithm.

nonce.description: Monotonically increasing per-signer nonce. A fresh nonce is required per request; replayed nonces are rejected with `INVALID_NONCE_ERROR`. See `docs/eip712.md`.

deadline.description: Unix seconds. EIP-712 signature validity deadline — signed into the `MassCancel` envelope and enforced by the API at entry.

MassCancelRequest.description: Request to cancel all orders matching the specified filters

cancelledCount.description: Number of orders that were cancelled

CancelAllAfterRequest.title: CancelAllAfterRequest
CancelAllAfterRequest.description: Arms, refreshes, or disarms the account-scoped cancel-all-after countdown (dead-man's-switch / cancel-on-disconnect). While armed, the server mass-cancels the account's open orders if the countdown is not refreshed with another `cancelAllAfter` call before `timeoutMs` elapses. Scope: all open orders EXCEPT protective stops (`STOP_LOSS` / `TAKE_PROFIT`) — the dead-man's-switch deliberately leaves protective stops in place (they are safety orders only the user should cancel explicitly), unlike an explicit `POST /v2/cancelAll` which cancels everything. Refresh is explicit only: order-entry traffic, WebSocket protocol pings, and app-level `ping`/`pong` frames do NOT refresh the countdown, and closing a WebSocket connection does NOT trigger it — only countdown expiry does. The switch is transport-agnostic (arm over REST, refresh over WS, or vice versa) and survives reconnects until it fires or is disarmed. `accountId`, `timeoutMs`, and `nonce` are signed via EIP-712 into the `CancelAllAfter(uint64 verifyingChainId, uint64 deadline, CancelAllAfterDetails cancelAllAfter)` envelope, where `CancelAllAfterDetails(uint64 accountId, uint64 timeoutMs, uint64 nonce)` and `deadline` is the signature validity. See `docs/eip712.md` for the signing algorithm and exact typehash strings.

accountId.description: Account ID whose open orders are covered by the countdown.

timeoutMs.description: Countdown duration in milliseconds. `0` disarms an armed countdown (no-op if none is armed); any non-zero value must be within [5000, 60000] and arms a fresh countdown of that duration, replacing any previously armed one (re-arming with the same value is the refresh/heartbeat). Values outside `{0} ∪ [5000, 60000]` are rejected with `INPUT_VALIDATION_ERROR` (the server validates the band). Signed into the `CancelAllAfter` envelope.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature over the `CancelAllAfter(uint64 verifyingChainId, uint64 deadline, CancelAllAfterDetails cancelAllAfter)` envelope, where `CancelAllAfterDetails(uint64 accountId, uint64 timeoutMs, uint64 nonce)`. See `docs/eip712.md` for the exact typehash string and signing algorithm.

nonce.description: Monotonically increasing per-signer nonce. A fresh nonce is required on every arm/refresh/disarm call; replayed nonces are rejected with `INVALID_NONCE_ERROR`.

signerWallet.description: Address of the signer that produced the EIP-712 signature. REQUIRED on `cancelAllAfter` (a deliberate hybrid: like `createOrder`, the claimed signer is declared and must equal the address recovered from the signature). This differs from the EIP-712 siblings `cancelOrder` / `cancelAll`, which carry NO `signerWallet` and recover the signer purely from the signature.

deadline.description: Unix seconds. EIP-712 signature validity deadline — signed into the `CancelAllAfter` envelope and enforced by the API at entry. NOT the countdown trigger time; the countdown is `timeoutMs` relative to server receipt.

accountId.description: Account ID the countdown applies to, echoed from the request.

timeoutMs.description: Effective countdown duration in milliseconds; `0` means the switch is now disarmed.

triggerAt.description: Advisory milliseconds POSIX timestamp at which the cancel-all fires if the countdown is not refreshed before then. The client sends a RELATIVE `timeoutMs`; the matching engine stamps the absolute deadline on ITS OWN clock (`now_me + timeoutMs`) and echoes it here, so the value is anchored to the engine clock (no API↔ME skew) — treat it as an estimate for display, not an exact wall-clock guarantee. Omitted when `timeoutMs` is `0` (disarmed).

qty.description: Aggregated quantity at this price level

bids.description: Bid side levels aggregated by price, sorted descending by price

asks.description: Ask side levels aggregated by price, sorted ascending by price
